title: Settings
publicIpHint: Panel IPv4; used in VPN Endpoint when no domain is selected
panelDomainHint: Example: vpn.example.com — A record must point to the public IP
panelDomainDnsHint: A record to the panel IP. HTTPS later needs an extra _acme-challenge TXT record in DNS (not only A).
httpsPortHint: Default 7443; used after the certificate is issued
timezoneHint: Used for dates and webhook timestamps in the panel
useDomainInEndpoint: Use domain instead of IP in VPN config Endpoint
awgConfigsHint: AWG configs, server keys, and subnets are configured on the Configs page.
letsEncryptEmailHint: Required when issuing and renewing the certificate
acmeTxtHint: An A record to the panel IP is not enough for Let's Encrypt. You will also need a _acme-challenge TXT record (name and value are shown after Issue / Renew). After adding it, confirm issuance in the panel.
httpsAccessHint: Access: https://your-domain:{port} (port can be changed in the Panel tab).
addTxtRecord: Add a TXT record in DNS
twoFactorHint: Extra code from an authenticator app (Google Authenticator, Aegis, etc.) at sign-in. If you lose access:
scanQrHint: Scan the QR in the app or enter the secret manually, then confirm with a code.
disable2faHint: Account password and app code are required so an attacker with an open session cannot remove protection in one click. Without this, disabling 2FA loses its purpose.
addTxtNotify: Add a TXT record in DNS
